chapter: Chapter 3 – The Making of a Global World

questions.question: How did the discovery of sea routes to Asia affect European trade?

options.B: It increased direct trade and reduced dependence on middlemen
options.C: It stopped the Silk Route completely

questions.explanation: Sea routes allowed Europeans to trade directly with Asia without relying on Arab intermediaries.

questions.question: Why was the Silk Route important in the pre-modern world?

options.A: It connected only China and India
options.B: It facilitated cultural and commercial exchanges
options.C: It was used only for military purposes

questions.explanation: The Silk Route enabled exchange of goods, ideas, religions, and cultures across continents.

questions.question: Which factor helped Europe emerge as a dominant trading power?
questions.explanation: Improved ships, navigation tools, and cartography helped European expansion.

questions.question: How did the Columbian Exchange impact global diets?

options.B: It introduced new crops and diversified diets
options.C: It ended food shortages in Asia

questions.explanation: New crops like potatoes and maize improved food supply in many regions.

questions.question: Why did European powers establish colonies in Asia and Africa?

options.B: To control trade routes and resources

questions.explanation: Colonies ensured access to raw materials and profitable markets.

questions.question: What was the main objective of mercantilism?

options.B: Accumulate wealth through favorable trade balance

questions.explanation: Mercantilism emphasized exports over imports to increase national wealth.

questions.question: How did indentured labour migration reflect global economic integration?

options.B: It connected labor markets across continents

questions.explanation: Indentured workers moved from India and China to plantations worldwide.

questions.question: What was the effect of the Great Depression (1929) on global trade?
questions.explanation: Global demand fell sharply, affecting agricultural and industrial economies.

questions.question: Why did Britain import raw cotton from India during industrialization?
questions.explanation: British industries depended on raw materials from colonies.

questions.question: How did railways contribute to globalization in colonies?

options.B: They connected production areas to ports

questions.explanation: Railways facilitated movement of goods and integration into world markets.

questions.question: What role did technology play in global trade expansion?
questions.explanation: Steamships and telegraphs accelerated global connections.

questions.question: Which country emerged as a major industrial power in the 19th century?
questions.explanation: Britain led industrialization and global trade expansion.

questions.question: Why did European settlers migrate to America and Australia?
questions.explanation: People sought better livelihoods and religious freedom.

questions.question: How did colonialism affect local industries in India?
questions.explanation: British imports damaged Indian handicraft industries.

questions.question: What was one consequence of the First World War on global trade?

options.A: Rise of the US as economic power

questions.explanation: The US emerged as a major lender and industrial power.

questions.question: Which system replaced slavery in many colonies?
questions.explanation: Indentured laborers were contracted to work on plantations.

questions.question: Why did food prices fall during the Great Depression?
questions.explanation: Excess production and reduced purchasing power lowered prices.

questions.question: What was the significance of the Suez Canal?

options.A: Shortened route between Europe and Asia

questions.explanation: It reduced travel time between Europe and Asia significantly.

questions.question: Which factor contributed to mass migration in the 19th century?
questions.explanation: All these factors encouraged global migration.

questions.question: How did colonies benefit European industries?
questions.explanation: Colonies were sources of raw materials and consumers of finished goods.

questions.question: Which organization was formed after WWII to stabilize global economy?
questions.explanation: IMF and World Bank were created under Bretton Woods system.

questions.question: Why did Britain focus on opium trade with China?
questions.explanation: Opium trade helped Britain correct trade imbalance with China.

questions.question: What was the Bretton Woods system?
questions.explanation: It established rules for international economic relations after WWII.

questions.question: Which event marked the beginning of economic nationalism in Europe?
questions.explanation: Countries raised tariffs and restricted imports during the Great Depression.

questions.question: How did steamships impact global trade?

options.B: Reduced time and cost of transportation

questions.explanation: Steamships made global trade faster and cheaper.
